source.section: AFCS (Automatic Flight Control System) > CPL (Coupled Steering) Mode > CPL TCN Sub-Mode Example

steps.action: Make sure your stick is centered, no force is applied on it and that you have an appropriate deadzone (see CONTROLS SETUP - WHAT YOU NEED MAPPED section).
Select the desired TACAN station using the UFC. Verify that the TACAN is on.
Activate the TCN navigation mode by pressing OSB next to TCN. TCN is boxed when selected.
If desired, click and hold (Left/Right mouse button) the CRS (Course Set) knob to set desired course (CSEL) to intercept the TACAN station from.
Press the AP button on the UFC to show the autopilot options.
Press the OSB adjacent “CPL” to activate coupled autopilot mode.
A colon will appear (“:CPL”) to indicate that it is active. “CPL TCN” will appear on both the HSI and HUD.
The autopilot will follow the selected TACAN station.
You can disengage autopilot by pressing the Paddle Switch on the control stick.
